review_note: Bridge candidate Duchy of Bavaria is a shared-HRE-parent false positive. Existing text support keeps the burgraviate as an imperial office/territory under the HRE; do not import a Bavaria intermediate without a child-level source.

Bridge candidate Duchy of Ferrara is a shared-Papal-parent false positive. Keep Papal States direct parentage until DUCHY models papal enclaves/theocratic territorial administration separately from parentage.

The accepted packet only supports the short endpoint under the Crown of Aragon. Do not infer Catalan Counties or Principality of Catalonia parentage without a bounded child-level source for this span.

Bridge candidate Duchy of Bavaria is a shared-HRE-parent false positive. Current source was scoped to Free County of Burgundy -> HRE only; Upper Burgundy, Arles, circle, and personal-union layers remain out of scope.

Accepted text support says Empuries was an apanage of the Crown of Aragon until 1402 annexation into the Principality of Catalonia. No intermediate parentage is importable for 1341..1402 from current evidence.
Bridge candidate Duchy of Bavaria is a shared-HRE-parent false positive. Current Flanders source is weak text support for HRE context only; French/Imperial fief split remains held.
Bridge candidate Anhalt-Bernburg is a shared-HRE-parent false positive. Burgundian Netherlands replacement already covers 1384..1482; post-1482 split needs separate source and relation modeling.

Bridge candidate Duchy of Bavaria is a shared-HRE-parent false positive. Current source only supports Geneva as an HRE county after Arles fell to the empire; predecessor and Savoyard layers remain unimported.
The accepted packet supports Girona under the Carolingian Empire. No duchy-rank intermediate is available; Spanish March or frontier-county semantics need a relation model before import.

Bridge candidate Duchy of Bavaria is a shared-HRE-parent false positive. Burgundian Netherlands replacement starts at 1433; earlier Holland needs child-level source review before adding any intermediate.
